programme.title: ABC News At Noon
programme.desc: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.

programme.title: National Press Club Address
programme.sub-title: Amanda Lacaze - Managing Director of Lynas Rare Earths
programme.desc: Amanda Lacaze, CEO and Managing Director of Lynas Rare Earths speaks about \.

programme.title: Media Watch
programme.desc: Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Fanny Lumsden, Shane Jacobson, Elly-may Barnes & Courtney Act
programme.desc: You want great guests? No problem. Adam, Myf and Alan are joined by Fanny Lumsden, Shane Jacobson, Elly-May Barnes and Courtney Act. You want a spine chilling closer? No problem again. Folk Bitch Trio will stun you.

programme.title: Anh's Brush With Fame
programme.desc: Anh paints celebrity chef Kylie Kwong, who was born to cook. She shares stories from her childhood in suburban Sydney and deeply spiritual return to her ancestral village in China.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: A homeowner takes a tiny rundown heritage-listed workers cottage in the middle of the dense inner city Sydney suburb of Newtown and transforms it into a beautiful, fully sustainable off-grid home. (Final).

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Detective Murdoch investigates the strange death of a man who either jumped from or pushed off of his balcony, which Murdoch finds suspicious; among his list of suspects are the deceaseds brothers, and the help have different stories.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: In this episode, we follow three stories of sons coming to terms with the past.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce returns with the specialists for a second visit to the Devon coast home of the cadets of the Britannia Royal Naval College, Dartmouth.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.sub-title: Australian Zoos, rap group Salt-N-Pepa, BoJack Horseman & Anna Pavlova
programme.desc: Trying to get their hands on Tom Gleeson's Big Brass Mug are experts in Australian zoos, rap group Salt-N-Pepa, animated series BoJack Horseman, and a fascinator-wearing fan of ballet superstar Anna Pavlova.

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News Victoria brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Tamara Oudyn (Sun-Thu) and Iskhandar Razak (Fri-Sat).

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: Gruen
programme.desc: Australia's favourite multi-award winning, multi-award losing, longest running, and most importantly, the only show about advertising is coming back for an 18th season.

programme.title: Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont Spelling Bee
programme.desc: The carryover champ defends their crown against Frank Woodley, Shabana Azeez and Sarah Keyworth, as Sam Campbell ramps up the chaos with \.

programme.title: Urzila
programme.desc: Urzila explodes over a courtesy wave, launches Hire-A-Lez, destroys self-help books, plays a menopausal cop, goes to Karen rehab and attends a cougar funeral.

programme.title: Bad Company
programme.desc: Julia is determined to get The Argyle on track with performance reviews, creating chaos as Margie's new play is about to open. Meanwhile Julia's son Ryan wanders the halls, catching Margie's eye.

programme.title: Planet America
programme.desc: Chas and John cover the latest from the Middle East, the Strait of Hormuz, the ceasefire and peace talks, plus King Charles' State Visit to mark the 250 years of American independence.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Jade Barker in Perth.

programme.title: The Business
programme.desc: Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing you up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, employees and business owners big and small. Presented by Kirsten Aiken.

programme.title: You Can't Ask That
programme.desc: From the pitfalls of 'pretty privilege', to teenage exploitation, unrealistic beauty standards, and mental health issues, eight models share the unvarnished truth about one of the most desired & least understood professions.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Detective Murdoch investigates the strange death of a man who either jumped from or pushed off of his balcony, which Murdoch finds suspicious; among his list of suspects are the deceaseds brothers, and the help have different stories.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: In this episode, we follow three stories of sons coming to terms with the past.

programme.title: Rage Guest Programmer
programme.desc: Australia's longest running music TV show, rage is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. We curate the best clips of all time to surprise and entertain you for hours.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.desc: Costa Georgiadis learns how to make a kokedama, Sophie Thomson visits a flower farm in the Adelaide Hills, Jane Edmanson shows us around her own courtyard garden and Millie Ross makes a portable potting bench.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Australian actor Erik Thomson joins Alice as together they make a warming one pot braise and Bannock bread that celebrates Erik's rich tapestry. And Alice treats Erik to cookies and milk - a treat he still loves today.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: News Breakfast (Sydney)
programme.desc: James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, overnight developments, interviews and new details on the top stories this morning, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Lillian Rangiah with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's going on this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Kathryn Robinson.

programme.title: Gruen
programme.desc: Australia's favourite multi-award winning, multi-award losing, longest running, and most importantly, the only show about advertising is coming back for an 18th season.

programme.title: Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont Spelling Bee
programme.desc: The carryover champ defends their crown against Frank Woodley, Shabana Azeez and Sarah Keyworth, as Sam Campbell ramps up the chaos with \.

programme.title: Urzila
programme.desc: Urzila explodes over a courtesy wave, launches Hire-A-Lez, destroys self-help books, plays a menopausal cop, goes to Karen rehab and attends a cougar funeral.

programme.title: ABC News At Noon
programme.desc: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: It's work experience week, and there's been a mix-up, meaning that Neil's at a paper and Will is at a garage. Mr Gilbert is unwilling to change things, so Will gets to see a place where he's \.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Astrid Jorgensen, Zoe Coombs Marr, Anthony Albanese & Ben Lee
programme.desc: Adam, Myf and Alan are joined by Astrid Jorgensen, Zoe Coombs Marr, Ben Lee and part-time DJ but full-time Prime Minister, Anthony Albanese. There'll be questions, a massive singalong and a peek into Ethel Chop's Op Shop.

programme.title: Anh's Brush With Fame
programme.desc: Actor David Wenham joins Anh with stories from his happy childhood, where his flair for performance flourished with the support and love from his deeply compassionate parents and siblings. (Final).

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Locked out of Melbourne's soaring housing market, Matt and Kate move to Tasmania to build two Earthships with no building experience.

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sam's career is in jeopardy when serial killer 'The Good Samaritan' resurfaces in Great Slaughter.

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The incredible true story of a man left on the front seat of a car in Northern Ireland and a woman abandoned six years later in a telephone box in the Republic of Ireland.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Yorkshire where a packed house awaits at Saltaire's Victoria Hall. Amongst the family treasures is a doodle by Lowry worth a small fortune.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.sub-title: Hello Kitty, Triumph motorcycles, Michael J. Fox & Gilbert and Sullivan
programme.desc: Tom Gleeson awards the Big Brass Mug to whichever expert best answers his Hard Quiz questions on their expert topic: Hello Kitty, Triumph motorcycles, actor Michael J. Fox, and composers Gilbert and Sullivan!

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News Victoria brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Tamara Oudyn (Sun-Thu) and Iskhandar Razak (Fri-Sat).

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: Back Roads continues along Central Australia's remote Binns Track, connecting people and cultures. Rae Johnston arrives just in time for one of the most action-packed events in the Northern Territory, the Harts Range Races.

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Graeme and Melanie built a bright red house on a tiny garage plot for their blended family. But constructing the basement proved a disaster. Eighteen months since his last visit, Kevin returns to see if they are finished.

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Renowned interviewer, Leigh Sales mentors autistic journalism students in The Assembly. In this episode, celebrated actor and producer Claudia Karvan shares insights into her work and relationships.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Craig Smart in Perth.

programme.title: The Business
programme.desc: Days out from the Federal Budget, Alicia Barry and The Business team bring you the latest in business, finance and economic news.

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sam's career is in jeopardy when serial killer 'The Good Samaritan' resurfaces in Great Slaughter.

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The incredible true story of a man left on the front seat of a car in Northern Ireland and a woman abandoned six years later in a telephone box in the Republic of Ireland.

programme.title: Rage Guest Programmer
programme.desc: Australia's longest running music TV show, rage is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. We curate the best clips of all time to surprise and entertain you for hours.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.desc: Costa Georgiadis finds cherry blossoms in Sydney, Millie Ross discovers the plants of the Victorian Goldfields, Jerry Coleby-Williams takes a deep dive into turmeric and ginger and Tino Carnevale grows spuds the easy way.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Appearance activist and award-winning writer, Carly Findlay joins Alice with a bunch of home-grown silver beat perfect for a scrunch pie. Alice surprises Carly with a vibrant salad as a shout out to Carly's love of colour.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: News Breakfast (Sydney)
programme.desc: James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, overnight developments, interviews and new details on the top stories this morning, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Lillian Rangiah with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's going on this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Kathryn Robinson.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: Ukrainian vet, Maria Mykytiuk, lived an idyllic life until Russia invaded her home of Bucha in 2022. She survived violence and the death of her best friend. Now Maria is safe in Australia, and using her voice to help Ukraine.

programme.title: A Symphonic Odyssey with Professor Brian Cox
programme.desc: Beginning in Ethiopia, Professor Brian Cox discovers how the universe played a key role in our ascent from apeman to spaceman by driving the expansion of our brains. But big brains alone did not get us to space. To reveal what did, Brian heads out of Africa to the ancient city of Petra in Jordan, where he unpicks the next part of our story - the birth of civilization - and then on to Kazakhstan, where he witnesses the return of astronauts from space and explains what took us from civilization to the stars.

programme.title: ABC News At Noon
programme.desc: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Renowned interviewer, Leigh Sales mentors autistic journalism students in The Assembly. In this episode, celebrated actor and producer Claudia Karvan shares insights into her work and relationships.

programme.title: Bad Company
programme.desc: Julia is determined to get The Argyle on track with performance reviews, creating chaos as Margie's new play is about to open. Meanwhile Julia's son Ryan wanders the halls, catching Margie's eye.
